key_insight: Adding site-projected orbital density of states (pDOS) to graph neural network node features gives the model direct electronic-structure context and substantially improves materials property prediction.
engineering_relevance: This offers a practical route to higher-accuracy, first-principles-informed screening of materials properties when crystal structure alone is not enough.
method: Compute pDOS fingerprints from DFT for each atomic site.
Fuse pDOS with Pettifor elemental embeddings before message passing in a graph neural network.
Use a spectral attention-gating mechanism to identify which orbitals and energy windows matter most.
evidence_signal: Compared with an elemental-descriptor baseline, pDOS augmentation reduced prediction error by 22.9% for superconducting Tc and 27.9% for optical dielectric constant ε∞, with gains comparable to doubling the training set.
watch_item: The benefit depends on training-set size and is weaker for smaller datasets, so pDOS feature length and data availability should be matched carefully.
paper_title: Machine Learning Materials Properties by Encoding Orbital-Projected Density of States
